have you ever wondered what the world around you is made

water in your bottle, the food in your lunch box, the clothes and

play with and even the stick you carry -- all of these are examples
of matter, which you have learnt in earlier grades.

enables students to systematically understand how elements are grouped according to
repeating property trends in the modern periodic table. this topic is essential for building
strong fundamentals in atomic structure, periodic trends, and chemical reactivity,
especially for students targeting exams like jee and neet. let's explore everything you need
to know about classification of elements and periodicity in properties with clear concepts,
modern trends, and useful study tips.

properties and trends of s block elements explained with examples
the s$ block elements are located in groups | and 2 of the periodic table and form a crucial
topic for jee aspirants. these include the alkali metals and alkaline earth metals,
characterized by the presence of their valence electrons in the s-orbital. their high
reactivity, especially with water and oxygen, shapes many basic chemical reactions in
inorganic chemistry. mastering the properties and trends of these elements is essential for
cracking jee main questions efficiently.
introduction to s block elements
s block elements occupy the extreme left side of the modern periodic table. group | is
called the alkali metals (li, na, k, rb, cs, fr), and group 2 is the alkaline earth metals (be, mg,
ca, sr, ba, ra). all these elements display metallic character and exhibit similar oute

fascinating facts about the periodic table

search your topic here.
the ordered arrangement of all chemical elements in order of increasing atomic
number--that is, the total number of protons in the atomic nucleus--is known as the
periodic table or the full periodic table of the elements.
the "periodic law,' which describes a recurring pattern in which elements in the
same column (group) have comparable properties, is present when the chemical
elements are grouped in this way. dmitry |. mendeleyev's original discovery, which
was made in the middle of the 19th century, has had immeasurable significance for
the advancement of chemistry.

list of p block elements in periodic table with group-wise properties
the p block elements in chemistry are crucial for jee main aspirants because they
encompass a vast range of elements with distinct properties, trends, and applications.
these elements are located on the right side of the periodic table, covering groups 13 to 18,
and include many of the main group elements such as nonmetals, metalloids, and noble
gases. understanding the electronic configuration, periodic trends, and specific exceptions
associated with p block elements lays a solid foundation for both conceptual and
application-based problems encountered in competitive exams like jee.

electronic configuration and periodic trends of d and f block
the d and f block elements chapter is a vital part of jee main chemistry, focusing on the
elements in the central region and bottom rows of the periodic table. these elements, with
their characteristic partially filled d or f orbitals, stand out for their variable properties,
colourful compounds, and essential industrial uses. mastering this chapter requires a solid
grip on periodic trends, electronic configuration patterns, and subtle differences between
d-block (transition metals) and f-block (inner transition metals such as lanthanides and
actinides). a clear understanding of this topic helps solve many tricky mcqs in jee main and
builds strong fundamentals for advanced chemistry questions.

title: Understanding the Modern Periodic Table and Its Elements

cleaned_text_snippet:
learn live online
understanding the modern periodic table and its
search your topic here. ee |
the modern periodic table is a crucial framework in chemistry, organizing all known
elements according to their atomic numbers. this systematic arrangement not only helps
students and scientists identify elements easily but also reveals repeating trends in their
chemical and physical properties. understanding the modern periodic table of elements is
fundamental for anyone exploring topics such as atomic structure, element classification,
and periodic trends in chemistry for class 10, class 1], and beyond.
development and structure of the modern periodic table
the modern periodic table arrangement is based on increasing atomic number, a principle
first established in the early 20th century. this layout replaced earlier versions, like
mendeleev's table that used atomic mass. the father of the modern periodic table is
usually recognized as henry moseley, who discovered that atomic number, not at
